This biographical article related to Chilean [equestrianism](/wiki/Equestrianism "Equestrianism") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Ren%C3%A9_Varas&action=edit).

(born 19 May 1936) is a Chilean [equestrian](/wiki/Equestrianism "Equestrianism"). He competed in [two events](/wiki/Equestrian_at_the_1972_Summer_Olympics "Equestrian at the 1972 Summer Olympics") at the [1972 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1972_Summer_Olympics "1972 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-SportsRef-1)

Evans, Hilary; Gjerde, Arild; Heijmans, Jeroen; [Mallon, Bill](/wiki/Bill_Mallon "Bill Mallon"); et al. From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Chilean equestrian (born 1936)

René Varas

| Personal information | |
| --- | --- |
| Nationality | Chilean |
| Born | (1936-05-19) 19 May 1936 (age 89) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Equestrian](/wiki/Equestrianism "Equestrianism") |
| Medal record  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | [Equestrian](/wiki/Equestrianism "Equestrianism") | | | | Representing  [Chile](/wiki/Chile "Chile") | | | | [Pan American Games](/wiki/Pan_American_Games "Pan American Games") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [1971 Cali](/wiki/1971_Pan_American_Games "1971 Pan American Games") | Team jumping | | |

**René Varas** (born 19 May 1936) is a Chilean [equestrian](/wiki/Equestrianism "Equestrianism"). He competed in [two events](/wiki/Equestrian_at_the_1972_Summer_Olympics "Equestrian at the 1972 Summer Olympics") at the [1972 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1972_Summer_Olympics "1972 Summer Olympics").[[1]](#cite_note-SportsRef-1)
